introduction: A parasitic protozoal infection caused by Giardia lamblia or Giardia intestinalis. Worldwide in distribution but more common in developing countries. Spread is by the faeco-oral route.

drug: Metronidazole (Adult: 2 g orally daily for 3 days; or 400 mg 8 hourly for 5 days; or 500 mg twelve hourly for 7 - 10 days; Child 1-3 years: 500 mg orally daily; 3-7 years: 600-800 mg daily; 7-10 years: 1 g daily for 3 days)
Tinidazole (Adult: 40 mg/kg orally as a single dose; or 2 g stat, repeat after 1 week, once if necessary; Child: 50 to 75 mg/kg as a single dose, repeat after 1 week)

complications: Diseases related to Vitamin B deficiency
